summary: Comprehensive backend test of the matches module (PROMPT 2). 36/36 active tests pass; 1 PRO-gating case manually verified via direct billing wipe (returns 403 with code=PLAN_PRO_REQUIRED). Covered: matches CRUD (SPLIT/FIXED/SPLIT_WITH_CASH/CASH_PAYS_ALL planned-price math, WEEKLY series creation, past-date rejection, player_limit bounds, patch recalc, cancel preserves doc, history includes cancelled), RSVP (1st-going SPLIT recalc → price=total, capacity → waitlist with positions, not_going auto-promotes waitlist, late_cancellation increments reliability_stats, RSVP on CANCELLED → 400, non-member → 403, bulk skips non-members, list/waitlist sort), APPROVAL flow (non-admin → pending; pending-requests; approve → going), Payments (SWC totals + currency=EUR + cash_contribution math, OVERPAID/UNPAID/PAID transitions with overpaid_to_cash, record-to-cash inserts INCOME + EXPENSE rows for SWC), Score (admin-only PRO setter), Results (set-goals blocked when player not in team), Teams draft (set-captains requires both going + reset gating, pick alternates BLUE↔RED, blocks picking other captain, lock toggle, reset clears teams + player_results), Recurrence (process-recurrence runs without error, stop-recurrence flips active=false, /series returns ordered list). Background recurrence loop confirmed alive across two backend restarts with no errors in supervisor logs.

minor.issue: Endpoint does not exist (returns 404 because /groups/{id} matches 'mine' as ObjectId attempt). Not part of this prompt's scope but confirms the only way to enumerate the OWNER's groups is via direct DB or /api/groups/public — please add a 'list my groups' route in a future iteration.

When status=PAID is sent with paid_amount BETWEEN 0 and amount (partial payment), the code stores it as PAID with no over/underpaid markers. Spec only explicitly mentions PAID/UNPAID/OVERPAID — current implementation treats partial as PAID. Confirm whether this is intended; outstanding_total still reflects the gap correctly because collected_total uses actual paid_amount.

Code returns Bulgarian error message for FREE plan cap on player_limit > 14, but does NOT silently cap (unlike create_match which silently caps). Inconsistent UX with create — patch is stricter (rejects) while create is lenient (caps). Recommend aligning either both reject or both cap.

action_items: (Optional) Decide partial-payment semantics (paid_amount < amount) — currently stored as PAID. Clarify in spec or add a PARTIAL status.
(Optional) Align FREE plan player_limit handling between POST (silent cap) and PATCH (400) for consistency.
(Optional) Add /api/groups/mine (or similar) to list groups the current user belongs to — currently no endpoint to enumerate OWNER's groups.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: planned_price_per_player computed correctly for all 4 pricing modes; recalculated on patch.
GOOD: SPLIT price_per_player auto-recalculates on every going RSVP / removal (verified: 280/1=280, 280/14=20).
GOOD: Late-cancellation reliability score uses (start_dt - utc_now) <= 2h window; increments late_cancellations and recomputes the bounded 0..100 score.
GOOD: rsvps unique index on (match_id, user_id) is sparse so multiple guest rsvps with user_id=null co-exist.
GOOD: PRO gating returns the structured detail {code: PLAN_PRO_REQUIRED, message: ...} as required (verified manually after wiping billing).
GOOD: serialize_doc converts all ObjectId/datetime fields recursively; payment per_player ids returned as strings.
MINOR: record-to-cash uses match.start_datetime.isoformat() but if Mongo returns naive datetime that branch still works. Consistent with Iteration 1 pattern.

MINOR: routes/matches.py is ~1100+ lines and tightly couples 6 sub-features (matches/RSVP/payments/score/teams/recurrence). Consider splitting into separate modules in a future refactor (matches_crud.py, rsvps.py, payments.py, teams.py, recurrence.py) for maintainability per the >700-line guideline.

MINOR: _create_or_update_rsvp re-reads going_count after computing target_status, which is correct, but if APPROVAL non-admin path returns 'pending' it skips the going/waitlist branch — verify it still increments waitlist_position consistently if the same user later approves into a full match (covered manually; OK).
MINOR: _apply_late_cancellation re-reads user from DB then writes the whole stats subdoc; race-condition safe enough for single-tenant tests but consider $inc with $set in production for atomicity.
test_credentials: Super tester +359888999999 via POST /api/auth/super-test-login. Test users created directly in MongoDB users + memberships collections to bypass OTP plumbing. JWT_SECRET from backend/.env used to mint tokens for non-admin/non-member users.
seed_data_creation: Tests rely on `mongosh footballchat --eval 'db.dropDatabase()' && supervisorctl restart backend && sleep 4` BEFORE the run (done by this iteration). Inside the suite, additional users + memberships are inserted directly via PyMongo (e.g. for waitlist, captains, approval flow). All created data is namespaced inside one transient group named 'MatchTest-<rand>'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run with: `mongosh footballchat --eval 'db.dropDatabase()' && sudo supervisorctl restart backend && sleep 4 && pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. The test_matches.py suite uses pytest module-level state (pytest.match1_id, pytest.weekly_match_id, pytest.cap2, etc.) so it must be run as a whole class-by-class (do not pass -k filters that skip earlier classes). The PRO-gating test is intentionally skipped because the same OWNER cannot create a 2nd group under FREE caps; manual verification was done by deleting billing for an existing group and confirming 403 + code=PLAN_PRO_REQUIRED.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in matches module. All endpoints behave per spec. Manual verification of PRO gating: wipe billing for any group → GET /api/matches/{mid}/payments returns 403 with detail={code:PLAN_PRO_REQUIRED, message:'Тази функция изисква PRO план'}.
